Rev. Father Piper officiated at

which was solemnized yesterday
morning at 6 o'clock in the st.
AIMS Catholic church.

MARRIED IN THE EAST.


News has been received in this
city announcing the marriage of
Frank Palmer, well known
scenic photographer of this city,
and Miss Francis Timisle, also an
artist, which was solemnized at
Fairberry, Neb.
MRS. REYNOLDS WILL SPEAK.


Mrs. M. J. Reynolds will occupy
the pulpit at the Hillyard Metho-
dist church Sunday evening, July
"a. She will speak on "Woman's
Influence in the State"

LARGE AFFAIR FOR WoMAN's


CLUB BENEFIT.


Since it was decided that the
Woman's club of this city should
have clubhouse, the members
have been untiring in their efforts
to obtain funds and have given
numerous social affairs both pub
lie and private.


"A Night in Japan to be given
next Friday evening by Mrs. Ike
Binnard at her home on S1227
Cedar street, will be one of the
most charming social affairs of the
week. A special feature will be
the splendid musical program
which has been arranged for the
occasion. =


The hostess will be assisted by
Mrs. Eugene De Hart. Mrs. b. M.
Francis, Mrs. Robert Kirk, Miss
Agatha Bieber, Miss Pauline Sill
yer, Miss Josie Ashiem and Miss
Fay Morvillius.


The following program will be
presented:

companist, Miss May Caroline Will
liams.

a Maying" (Nevin), Mrs. n. A..
